plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Trim concentrated positions (SMH, CRWD) and SPY to reduce single-name and sector risk.
Add to income ETF (QQQI) to boost steady distributions.
Buy XLK, a quality tech index ETF, for broad exposure.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a large cash buffer, aiming for lower volatility.
conservative.read: This plan focuses on capital preservation and reducing risk. Expect a lower target return, around 1.5% per month, with significantly reduced potential for large drawdowns. It prioritizes stability over aggressive growth.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce overall leverage.
Reduce concentration in SPY and SMH.
Add to CRWD, a strong performer, and buy XLK for quality tech exposure.
Initiate a small position in ALAB, a high risk/reward setup.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction, aiming for steady progress.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for risk-first growth, targeting around 2.5% per month. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality names and a high potential setup. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate, with a focus on disciplined risk management.

plan: Increase exposure to leveraged ETFs SOXL and SPAL, and strong sector SMH.
Add to CRWD, a top performer, and initiate a position in MRVL.
Trim SPY and QQQI to reallocate capital to higher-conviction plays.
This plan embraces concentration and leverage for maximum growth potential.
Strict stop discipline is critical due to amplified risk and leverage decay.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for stretch growth, targeting around 4% per month by leaning into leverage and concentration. It significantly increases exposure to leveraged ETFs and high-scoring setups. Expect higher potential upside but also substantially larger drawdowns and amplified losses due to leverage and daily rebalancing of leveraged ETFs.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and high-beta tech/semis (SOXL, SPAL, SMH, CRWD).
Significantly increase QQQI to maximize income generation.
Trim SPY and buy XLK for broad, less volatile exposure.
This plan prioritizes stable monthly distributions and capital preservation.
It aims for low drawdowns by focusing on income-generating assets and broad market exposure.
income.read: This income-focused plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and capital preservation, targeting around 1% per month. It shifts heavily into income-generating ETFs and broad market exposure, cutting high-beta and leveraged assets. While aiming for low drawdowns, NAV can still decline in market selloffs, and distributions are variable.
